item.title: Matsui Securities in Talks With Finance Firms on Capital Tie-Up
item.description: Matsui Securities Ltd., one of Japan’s pioneers for online trading, has begun talks with several major domestic financial institutions over a possible capital alliance, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Czech Rates to Stay Put as Policymakers Lean on Inflation Buffer
item.description: Czech policymakers are poised to keep interest rates on hold as inflation running below target provides a cushion against the immediate impact of surging oil costs.

item.title: India’s Net Forward Dollar Sales to Aid Rupee Near $100 Billion
item.description: India ramped up use of a key tool for defending the rupee to record levels as the currency weakened to an all-time low against the dollar, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Emerging Assets Fall for First Time This Week as Oil Prices Jump
item.description: Emerging market equities and currencies fell for the first time this week as oil prices surged after attacks on some of the Middle East’s most important energy facilities.

item.title: Powell Digs In as War Forces a New Path for the Fed
item.description: The embattled chairman raises the stakes against prosecution.

item.title: Bank of Japan Decision Puts Yen-160 Watch in Focus, Analysts Say
item.description: The Bank of Japan’s widely expected decision to hold its benchmark rate amid risks from the Iran war left the yen little changed, though analysts said Governor Kazuo Ueda’s remarks later would be key to whether the currency would swing past 160.

item.title: JPMorgan’s First Taiwan ETF in Over Decade Faces Crowded Market
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management is issuing its first Taiwan-focused wealth management product in more than a decade, joining global rivals rushing into one of Asia’s hottest exchange-traded funds markets.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Says Sell Asian Stock Rally on Iran War Impact
item.description: Morgan Stanley recommended investors sell into this week’s rally in Asian equities, warning of a deeper market downturn as energy prices surge.

item.title: Xiaomi Shares Jump on AI Model Buzz, Upcoming SU7 Model Facelift
item.description: Xiaomi Corp. shares rallied Thursday after the company released its latest artificial intelligence models and as it prepared to launch a refreshed version of its SU7 electric vehicle.

item.title: Copper Gives Up 2026 Gains as Iran War Roils Metals Markets
item.description: Copper fell to the lowest since December as the worsening war in the Middle East pushed energy prices higher and increased the risk of damage to the global economy.

item.title: Zijin’s Investment Unit Aims to Expand Strategic Metal Portfolio
item.description: Zijin Mining Group, China’s biggest gold and copper producer, wants to invest in metals like tungsten and uranium, as the global bid to secure critical minerals drives prices higher.

item.title: Yen Holds Gain Against Dollar After BOJ Keeps Rates Steady
item.description: The yen held onto gains after the Bank of Japan kept its benchmark rate unchanged amid an escalating war in Iran.

item.title: UBP Sees Decade‑Long Yuan Rally on Fundamentals, Policy Reforms
item.description: Union Bancaire Privée has made the Chinese yuan one of its strongest bets, expecting the currency to keep rising against the dollar over the next decade due to improving fundamentals and policy reforms.

item.title: India Nifty’s Gains Look Fragile on Soaring Crude, HDFC Bank Board Exit
item.description: Local shares have shed more than $600 billion in market value this year.

item.title: BOJ Holds Rates Steady as Iran Conflict Clouds Economic Outlook
item.description: The Bank of Japan kept its policy rate unchanged as uncertainty surrounding the Middle East conflict clouds the economic outlook.

item.title: Lynas Expands Rare Earths Clout With First Batch of Samarium
item.description: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd. has started producing samarium — a metal used across advanced industries — at its plant in Malaysia, bolstering the company’s standing as an alternative to China in supplying critical materials.

item.title: Alibaba AI Business Is ‘Free Call Option,’ First Eagle Fund Says
item.description: Alibaba Group Holdings Ltd.’s share price is only factoring in the company’s e-commerce business, not its artificial intelligence potential, says the manager of a $17 billion fund.

item.title: Gas Traders Assess Damage at World’s Top LNG Plant in Qatar
item.description: Natural gas traders braced for market turbulence after Qatar reported “extensive damage” to the complex hosting the world’s largest liquefied natural gas facility following multiple Iranian strikes.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Says Ready to Take Action on Forex at Any Time
item.description: Japan’s finance minister said authorities are fully prepared to take action against currency moves if needed at any time, as traders test the dollar’s upside against the yen ahead of a Bank of Japan policy decision later in the day.

item.title: Elliott Builds Stake in Invisalign-Maker Align Technology
item.description: Elliott Investment Management has built a significant stake in Align Technology Inc., the maker of Invisalign teeth-straightening products, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Philippine Peso Drops Past Key 60-Per-Dollar Level on Oil Risk
item.description: The Philippine peso fell past the key level of 60 per dollar as elevated oil prices weigh on the country’s economic outlook.

item.title: Chinese Funds Favor Petchems Over Metals as War Roils Futures
item.description: Chinese investors are favoring wagers on petrochemicals over base metals, as they trade the fallout from the war in Iran on local futures markets.

item.title: Philippine Wealth Fund Likes Physical Assets as Iran War Rages
item.description: The Philippines’ sovereign wealth fund says investing in physical assets like metals makes increasing sense against the backdrop of a war in Iran that’s playing havoc with the nation’s fuel market.

item.title: JPMorgan Shut China Tycoon’s Account That Made Millions for Bank
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has shut the private banking account of Tang Hao, a Chinese investor who rose to prominence after taking a multibillion-dollar stake in one of the world’s top-performing stocks, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Australia Appoints Fuel Czar to Tackle Iran War Disruptions
item.description: Australia appointed former energy regulator Anthea Harris as a new fuel czar to coordinate the response to price spikes and supply chain disruptions as a result of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Bolivia Wins Credit Rating Upgrade After Coupon Payment Pledge
item.description: Moody’s Ratings lifted Bolivia’s credit rating following a pledge by the government it will be able to meet dollar bond coupon payments this month.

item.title: Australian Hiring Stays Strong, Backing Hawkish RBA Stance
item.description: Australian unemployment stayed relatively low in February as job gains continued, reinforcing the Reserve Bank’s view that the economy remains resilient enough to withstand tighter monetary policy.

item.title: Mexico REIT Fibra Macquarie Set for Bidding War After New Offers
item.description: Two Mexican real estate investment trusts have said they plan to bid for peer Fibra Macquarie, one day after Fibra Prologis shareholders signed off on plans to buy it.

item.title: RBA Warns of Global Financial Stability Risks as Iran War Rages
item.description: Australia’s central bank said local households and firms are broadly well placed to cope with recent interest-rate increases and surging fuel prices from the escalating Middle East conflict, while highlighting the potential for an unexpected shock as a result of elevated international risk.

item.title: Japan Stocks Fall After Oil Price Surges, Fed Holds Key Rate
item.description: Japanese stocks fell after oil prices surged again with new attacks on Middle East energy infrastructure and the US Federal Reserve said it won’t cut interest rates again until inflation resumes cooling.
